title: Monitor and Control Remote Access Sessions
description: monitor and control remote access sessions

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Permitted
checks.purpose: Whether remote access is permitted is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Types of Permitted Remote Access are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of permitted remote access types is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify that RDP access is restricted to named groups, NLA is enforced, Windows Defender Firewall restricts RDP to approved subnets, and remote access requires MFA or certificate-based authentication

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Monitored
checks.purpose: Verify that Windows audit policy captures logon and logoff events for remote sessions and that RDP session audit logging is active

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Permitted
checks.purpose: Whether remote access is permitted is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Types of Permitted Remote Access are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of permitted remote access types is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify that RDP access is restricted to named AD groups, NLA is enforced, Windows Defender Firewall restricts RDP to approved subnets, and RDP session limits are configured

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Monitored
checks.purpose: Verify that Windows advanced audit policy captures logon, logoff, and account logon events for remote sessions and that RDP audit logging is active with sufficient log retention

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Permitted
checks.purpose: Whether remote access is permitted is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Types of Permitted Remote Access are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of permitted remote access types is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH is restricted to named users or groups, root login is disabled, key-based authentication is enforced, and firewall rules limit SSH to approved source IPs

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Monitored
checks.purpose: Verify that auditd captures SSH session events, auth log is active and non-empty, and PAM session logging is configured to record remote access activity

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Permitted
checks.purpose: Whether remote access is permitted is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Types of Permitted Remote Access are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of permitted remote access types is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH is restricted to named users or groups, root login is disabled, key-based authentication is enforced, and firewall rules limit SSH to approved source IPs

checks.name: Remote Access Sessions are Monitored
checks.purpose: Verify that auditd captures SSH session events, auth log is active and non-empty, and PAM session logging is configured to record remote access activity

remediation: Require all remote access to use a VPN with multi-factor authentication rather than exposing RDP or SSH directly to the internet. On Windows, restrict RDP access to named Active Directory groups and enforce Network Level Authentication via Group Policy under Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services. Configure Windows Defender Firewall to block inbound RDP from non-approved source addresses. Enable audit logging for logon and logoff events so all remote sessions are recorded. On Linux, restrict SSH access via AllowUsers or AllowGroups in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, disable root login, and review auth log regularly.
